title: Cursor Pagination, Ordering, Filters, and Snapshot Behavior · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for cursor pagination, ordering, filters, and snapshot behavior and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for stable bounded reader timeline query. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Choose a deterministic immutable-enough order with unique tie-breaker and document updates that can move entries.
Encode version, tenant, sort values, direction, filters, expiry, and integrity without exposing authority or accepting arbitrary SQL.
Build forward and backward keyset predicates that match sort direction, null policy, indexes, and limit-plus-one boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
